note: Labelled question set for R7 threshold calibration and parser regression (ADR 012, open-questions #19). Categories: 'variant' = registry-internal variant choice, must resolve to the canonical default WITHOUT clarifying (docs/05 canonical-default policy); 'ambiguous' = user-facing ambiguity, must exit to clarification, never a best guess; 'refusal' = must classify forecast/causal/out-of-scope/compound; 'smalltalk' = not a data question; 'open_range' (WP14, open-questions #55) = open-ended period phrasings that must parse to since/last_n/now_vs_ago and resolve WITHOUT clarifying — the validation pass's V01/V02/V28 phrasings verbatim. The benchmark tasks B1-B20 and the un-disambiguated B3/B5 variants are NOT duplicated here — they come from tests/helpers/benchmark-intents.ts. referenceDate is chosen so that 'vorige maand' (2026MM07) lies beyond the loaded CPI slice (freshest: 2026MM06) — B20 must exercise the freshness refusal, not an answer. Labels are product-policy judgments; changing one is a reviewed decision, never a way to make a red run green. Label decisions so far (rationale in ADR 012): r-autos expects a measure CLARIFICATION rather than a scope refusal — for near-boundary topics both are honest (no number, topic flagged as not loaded); the crisp canonical behaviors are pinned by B15 (clarify) and B17 (scope refusal). WP14 label decisions (rationale in ADR 019): open-range intents label the FULL requested range even when its start predates loaded coverage (os-v28, os-inwoners-10jr) — no clamping in the parser, the query layer's slice/publication refusals stay the single honest source; V02-style 'vergeleken met' labels derivation 'none' (difference only on an explicit 'met hoeveel'); 'date_range' = explicit day/month boundaries (#77, ADR 023) incl. the misaligned-days clarification and the bare-years no-regression pin.

cases.note: Live-BISTABLE case (measured s50 and again s54: refusal<->clarification across records/evals, both honest for a near-boundary topic — ADR 012 note). Label = the ORIGINAL policy choice (clarification) and matches the committed v6 fixture; expect this case to flake in live evals until the vocabulary changes the balance again. The crisp canonical behaviors stay pinned by B15 (clarify) and B17 (scope refusal).

cases.question: Hoeveel stikstof stoot de landbouw uit?
Wat was de inflatie in 2024 en hoeveel woningen waren er toen?
Hallo! Wat kun je allemaal?

Wat is de werkloosheid in Nederland sinds 2010?
Wat is de huidige inflatie in Nederland vergeleken met vijf jaar geleden?
cases.note: Re-labelled 'none' -> 'difference' (session 50, 2026-07-17): after the coverage-sprint #2/#3 vocabulary batch the model STABLY reads the compare-ask as the registered difference derivation (2x record, 0.92 conf). Verified BETTER product behavior before re-labelling: 'difference' is the B13-registered two-period delta (both cells shown + CC BY-marked derived value), never a refusal.

Hoeveel inwoners had Nederland de afgelopen tien jaar?

Maak een grafiek van de inflatie van 1 januari 2022 tot en met 31 december 2022

Hoe ontwikkelde de werkloosheid zich van 1 januari 2024 tot en met 30 juni 2025?
cases.note: RELABELLED session 54 (2026-07-18, KW->MM): with the monthly key (80590ned) in the vocabulary, month-precise boundaries ('1 januari 2024 t/m 30 juni 2025') route to the MONTHLY series at 0.92 - a finer-grain honest reading (same seasonally-adjusted definition). Bare 'werkloosheid' asks (v-werkloosheid-nu, os-v01, os-v28) still resolve to the quarterly canonical default - #165 discipline measured intact on record 1.

cases.question: Wat was de inflatie van 15 januari 2022 tot 20 maart 2022?
Wat was de inflatie van 1 januari 2022 tot en met 31 januari 2022?
cases.note: Label corrected after the first v5 probe run (2026-07-05): the model parses the date_range correctly but at confidence 0.85 — the mild-doubt band — because 'van 1 t/m 31 januari' is a genuinely odd way to ask about one month; the R7 rule-3 echo clarification ('Bedoel je ... over de periode ...?') is the calibrated policy working as designed, so the label follows the policy, not vice versa — the same label-follows-policy move as ADR 019 §7's reviewed label correction in WP14 (c-b15-option-only's axis pin). Resolution of a single-month date_range at answerable confidence stays pinned hermetically in tests/answer/intent-resolve.test.ts.

cases.question: Hoe ontwikkelde de inflatie zich van 2020 tot en met 2024?
Hoe ontwikkelde de inflatie zich van maart 2022 tot september 2022?
cases.note: Pins the month-only bare-'tot' reading end-to-end against the live model. The review (2026-07-05) demanded this shape be live-scored; the model read it INCLUSIVE (t/m september) at 0.95, stable x3, while correctly keeping day-precise 'tot 1 januari 2023' exclusive — OWNER DECISION (Stefan, 2026-07-05, in-session): accept the everyday-inclusive reading for month-only bare 'tot'. ADR 023 alternative 4 records the reversal; the prompt rule's wording fix rides the next prompt-changing WP (any registry change re-records everything anyway). If this case starts flipping toward exclusive, that ADR trigger has fired.

cases.question: Wat deed de omzet van de detailhandel in juni 2023?
cases.note: Session-54 record-1 finding: the original phrasing ('...ten opzichte van een jaar eerder') made the model emit a two-period comparison, and 2026 periods intermittently read as forecast to the deliberately date-free prompt — labelled cases pin ROUTING, so they use clearly-past periods and plain phrasings (the CC tasks pin the 2026 cells hermetically instead).

cases.question: Wat deed de omzet van supermarkten in mei 2025?
Wat was de consumptiegroei van huishoudens in december 2025?
cases.note: Record-1: the looser 'consumptie van huishoudens' phrasing parsed correctly but at 0.85 -> clarification (below the 0.9 R7 floor); 'consumptiegroei' names the growth reading directly.

cases.question: Wat is de gemiddelde huizenprijs nu?
cases.note: #165 discipline pin, session 54: the bare national 'huizenprijs' ask STAYS on the monthly national key even now the per-gemeente key (83625NED) exists.
